title: Civic Pass API Vocabulary
description: Domain vocabulary for the Civic Pass Customer API covering key terms, concepts, and values used across the API surface.

terms.definition: A non-transferable on-chain token (attestation) issued to a user's blockchain wallet that represents verified user attributes such as identity, liveness, or location. Cannot be transferred between wallets.
An on-chain address identifying the type of pass and the verification requirements. Each gatekeeper network corresponds to a specific verification tier (e.g., uniqueness, liveness, identity).
The blockchain programming model. Civic supports 'solana' and 'ethereum' (including EVM-compatible L2 networks).
A specific network within a blockchain type, such as a mainnet or testnet. For example, 'sepolia' is an Ethereum testnet; 'mainnet-beta' is Solana mainnet.
A user's blockchain wallet address. For Solana this is a base58-encoded public key; for Ethereum it is a hex-encoded EVM address.
The current status of a Civic Pass as tracked by Civic's off-chain system.

values.description: The pass is valid and active.
The pass is temporarily suspended; can be unfrozen.
The pass has been permanently revoked; cannot be reissued to the same wallet on the same chain/network.
A pass issuance has been initiated but not yet confirmed on-chain.

terms.definition: The confirmed state of the Civic Pass as recorded on the blockchain. May lag behind 'state' during asynchronous blockchain operations.
A UUID provided by Civic after a user submits their PII for identity verification. Used to retrieve verified user information via the PII request endpoints.

Personally Identifiable Information. Civic collects and verifies user PII (e.g., name, date of birth, document number) off-chain and provides it to partners via the /piirequest endpoint. Sensitive PII is never stored on-chain.
A PII request status value indicating the partner has approved the receipt of user PII and authorized issuance of the Civic Pass.
A PII request status value indicating the partner has rejected the user's PII and blocked issuance of the Civic Pass.
A Unix epoch timestamp (seconds) after which the Civic Pass is considered expired. Can be set via PATCH to force a user to re-verify.

An immutable log entry recording every action taken on a Civic Pass, including issuance, freezing, unfreezing, expiration changes, and revocation. Each event has a type, timestamp, and optional blockchain transaction reference.

The authentication flow used by the Civic Pass Customer API. Partners obtain an access token from https://auth0.civic.com/oauth/token using their client_id and client_secret, then include the token as a Bearer token in API requests.
An MCP (Model Context Protocol) gateway that provides AI agents with secure, audited access to 80+ external tools through a single Streamable HTTP endpoint at app.civic.com/hub/mcp.
OAuth 2.0 / PKCE-based authentication service for web and mobile apps, supporting email, Google, and Web3 wallet sign-in with embedded wallet provisioning on Solana and EVM chains.
